The `tokenize()` method is on `AsyncGPTModel` and accepts messages compatible with the SDK's message format. The implementation creates a fresh model via `_getModel()`, calls `tokenize()`, and returns the count.

title: YC SDK v0.20.2: Complete API capabilities discovered
content:
Full exploration of `yandex_ai_studio_sdk==0.20.2` at `/Users/vgoshev/Development/NotA/gromozeka/venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yandex_ai_studio_sdk/`.

**Key capabilities the SDK exposes but our provider (`yc_sdk_provider.py`) does not use:**
- Chat domain (`sdk.chat.completions`) — HTTP/OpenAI-compatible API, supports `reasoning_mode` as 'low'/'medium'/'high' and `extra_query` for arbitrary model params
- SpeechKit (TTS + STT) with streaming, bidirectional streaming, and deferred recognition
- Text embeddings (`sdk.models.text_embeddings`) with well-known aliases 'doc' → 'text-search-doc', 'query' → 'text-search-query'
- Text classifiers (zero-shot + few-shot)
- Full tool/function calling (`FunctionTool`, `SearchIndexTool`, `GenerativeSearchTool`)
- Structured output via `response_format` (literal 'json', JSON Schema dict, or auto-extract from pydantic model class)
- Tokenization via `model.tokenize(messages)` → `tuple[Token, ...]`
- Batch processing, tuning/fine-tuning, search API, search indexes, datasets management
- LangChain integration via `model.langchain()`
- Custom retry policy via `RetryPolicy(max_attempts, initial_backoff, ...)`

**Architecture:**
- Two API tracks: gRPC-based (`sdk.models.completions`) and HTTP/OpenAI-compatible (`sdk.chat.completions`)
- gRPC track supports deferred operations (run_deferred → Operation[T]), chat track does not
- gRPC track has `ReasoningMode` (DISABLED, ENABLED_HIDDEN), chat track has `ChatReasoningMode` (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH)
- Image generation always deferred (no sync `.run()`)
- Every service available in sync (AIStudio) and async (AsyncAIStudio) variants
- Sync variants run async implementation in background thread via `run_sync()`

title: YC SDK missing OpenAI-standard generation parameters
content:
The `yandex_ai_studio_sdk` v0.20.2 does NOT support several standard OpenAI generation parameters. This matters for provider abstraction design at `lib/ai/providers/`:

**Not supported (neither gRPC nor HTTP):**
- `stop` sequences — no way to specify stop tokens
- `top_p` (nucleus sampling)
- `frequency_penalty` and `presence_penalty`
- `logprobs` and `top_logprobs`
- `seed` (except image generation model config has `seed`)
- `n` (number of completions)
- `user` field

**Supported:**
- `temperature` (float 0-1)
- `max_tokens`
- `stream` (via `run_stream()`)
- `response_format` ('json', JSON Schema, or auto-extract from pydantic)
- `tools` / `tool_choice` / `parallel_tool_calls`
- `reasoning_mode` (gRPC: DISABLED/ENABLED_HIDDEN; HTTP: low/medium/high)

title: YC SDK: chat.completions HTTP API supports extra_query for arbitrary params
content: The `yandex_ai_studio_sdk._chat.completions.config.ChatModelConfig` has an `extra_query: QueryType | None = None` field. This allows passing arbitrary additional parameters to the model via the HTTP/OpenAI-compatible API, which the gRPC-based `sdk.models.completions.GPTModelConfig` does NOT support. This is a Yandex Cloud-specific extension not present in the OpenAI API spec. Import: `from yandex_ai_studio_sdk._chat.completions.config import ChatModelConfig`.

title: YC SDK exception hierarchy
content:
All exceptions exported from `yandex_ai_studio_sdk.exceptions`:
- `AIStudioError` — base for all SDK errors
- `AIStudioConfigurationError` — invalid configuration
- `AioRpcError` — gRPC error with .endpoint, .auth, .stub_class, .client_request_id attributes for debugging; extends grpc.aio.AioRpcError
- `AsyncOperationError` — base for operation errors
- `WrongAsyncOperationStatusError` — operation in wrong state
- `RunError` — operation failed (.code, .message, .details, .operation_id)
- `DatasetValidationError` — dataset validation failed
- `TuningError` — tuning task failed
- `UnknownEndpointError` — unknown service endpoint
- `HttpSseError` — HTTP SSE streaming error

title: YC SDK auth auto-detection order
content:
When `auth=None` or undefined is passed to `AIStudio()`/`AsyncAIStudio()`, the SDK auto-detects credentials in this priority order:
1. `YC_API_KEY` env var → `APIKeyAuth`
2. `YC_IAM_TOKEN` env var → `IAMTokenAuth` (static token)
3. `YC_OAUTH_TOKEN` env var → `OAuthTokenAuth` (auto-refreshes IAM token every 3600s)
4. `YC_METADATA_ADDR` env var or default metadata service (169.254.169.254) → `MetadataAuth`
5. `YC_TOKEN` env var → `EnvIAMTokenAuth` (read per-request)
6. `yc` CLI installed + configured → `YandexCloudCLIAuth`

All token-based auth classes that auto-refresh extend `RefresheableIAMTokenAuth`. Full list from `yandex_ai_studio_sdk.auth`: `APIKeyAuth`, `IAMTokenAuth`, `EnvIAMTokenAuth`, `OAuthTokenAuth`, `YandexCloudCLIAuth`, `MetadataAuth`, `NoAuth`.

title: YC SDK model gallery: available models, URIs, context sizes
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026). Models available via the SDK, their URI patterns, context lengths, and supported APIs:

| Model | URI | Context | Available APIs |
|---|---|---|---|
| YandexGPT Pro 5.1 | `gpt://<folder>/yandexgpt-5.1` | 32k | Text gen, OpenAI-compatible |
| YandexGPT Pro 5 | `gpt://<folder>/yandexgpt-5-pro` | 32k | Text gen, OpenAI-compatible |
| YandexGPT Lite 5 | `gpt://<folder>/yandexgpt-5-lite` | 32k | Text gen, OpenAI-compatible |
| Alice AI LLM | `gpt://<folder>/aliceai-llm` | 32k | Text gen, OpenAI-compatible |
| DeepSeek V3.2 | `gpt://<folder>/deepseek-v32` | 128k | OpenAI-compatible only |
| Qwen3 235B | `gpt://<folder>/qwen3-235b-a22b-fp8` | 256k | OpenAI-compatible only |
| gpt-oss-120b | `gpt://<folder>/gpt-oss-120b` | 128k | OpenAI-compatible only |
| gpt-oss-20b | `gpt://<folder>/gpt-oss-20b` | 128k | OpenAI-compatible only |
| Qwen3.5 35B | `gpt://<folder>/qwen3.5-35b-a3b-fp8` | 256k | OpenAI-compatible only |
| Gemma 3 27B | `gpt://<folder>/gemma-3-27b-it` | 128k | OpenAI-compatible (expires May 15, 2026) |
| Fine-tuned YandexGPT Lite | `gpt://<folder>/yandexgpt-lite/latest@<suffix>` | 32k | Text gen, OpenAI-compatible |
| YandexART 2.0 | `art://<folder>/yandex-art-2.0` | 500 chars | Image gen APIs |
| Realtime | `gpt://<folder>/speech-realtime-250923` | 32k | Realtime API |

**Model versioning:** `model_version='latest'` by default. URI pattern: `gpt://<folder_id>/<model_name>/<version>`.

**Note:** Gemma 3 27B supports vision (Base64-encoded images, 256 tokens per image, auto-scaled to 896px longer side) but expires May 15, 2026.

**Note:** "OpenAI-compatible only" means the model is only accessible via the HTTP/OpenAI-compatible API (`sdk.chat.completions`), not via the gRPC completions API (`sdk.models.completions`).

title: YC SDK: AsyncGPTModel configuration and execution methods
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026). The `AsyncGPTModel` (accessed via `sdk.models.completions()`) has a rich set of configuration and execution methods:

**`model.configure()` parameters:**
- `temperature` — float 0-1, sampling temperature
- `max_tokens` — int, max tokens in response
- `reasoning_mode` — int/str/ReasoningMode, enables chain-of-thought internal reasoning before responding
- `response_format` — 'json' / JsonSchemaResponseType dict / pydantic model type, for structured output
- `tools` — Sequence[FunctionTool] | FunctionTool, for function calling
- `parallel_tool_calls` — bool, default True, allow parallel tool calls
- `tool_choice` — 'none'/'auto'/'required' / dict / FunctionTool, strategy for choosing tools

**Execution modes on AsyncGPTModel:**
- `model.run(messages, timeout=180)` — synchronous request, returns GPTModelResult
- `model.run_stream(messages, timeout=180)` — streaming, returns AsyncIterator[GPTModelResult] with PARTIAL status
- `model.run_deferred(messages, timeout=60)` — deferred/async operation, returns AsyncOperation
- `model.attach_deferred(operation_id, timeout=60)` — attach to existing deferred op
- `model.tokenize(messages, timeout=60)` — tokenize input, returns tuple[Token, ...]
- `model.tune_deferred(...)` — start fine-tuning
- `model.tune(..., poll_timeout=259200, poll_interval=60)` — fine-tune with polling
- `model.attach_tune_deferred(task_id)` — attach to existing tuning task
- `model.langchain(model_type='chat', timeout=60)` — get LangChain-compatible model

**Result structure:** GPTModelResult with .alternatives list; each Alternative has .role, .text, .status (UNSPECIFIED, PARTIAL, TRUNCATED_FINAL, FINAL, CONTENT_FILTER). Usage tracking via result.usage (.input_text_tokens, .completion_tokens, .total_tokens).

**Input messages** can be: str, dict ({"role": "...", "text": "..."}), TextMessage object, or Iterable of any of these.

title: YC SDK: structured output / JSON mode with schema support
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026). The SDK supports two modes for structured output via `response_format` in `model.configure()`:

**1. Free-form JSON (any structure):**
```
model = model.configure(response_format="json")
```
API equivalent: `"jsonObject": true`. Must mention JSON in the prompt for best results.

**2. Strict JSON schema (recommended for function calling):**
```
model = model.configure(
    response_format={
        "json_schema": {
            "properties": {
                "day": {"title": "Day", "type": "integer"},
                "month": {"title": "Month", "type": "string"},
                "year": {"title": "Year", "type": "integer"},
            },
            "required": ["day", "month", "year"],
            "type": "object",
        }
    }
)
```
Also accepts **pydantic models** directly as response_format. A strict schema is recommended when using function calling with external tools.

**Run configuration overrides:** The Assistants API also supports `custom_response_format` as a run override parameter.

title: YC SDK: tool calling with generative_search and search_index tools
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026). The SDK's `sdk.tools` domain provides:

**Function tools:** `sdk.tools.function` — creates function tools from Python callables (auto-generates tool definitions).

**GenerativeSearch tool:** `sdk.tools.generative_search(description, site, host, url, enable_nrfm_docs, search_filters)` — wraps Yandex Search API as an LLM tool. Scope limiting via mutually exclusive params: `site`, `host`, `url`. `search_filters` accepts DateFilterType, FormatFilterType, LangFilterType.

**SearchIndex tool:** `sdk.tools.search_index(indexes, max_num_results, rephraser, call_strategy)` — RAG over uploaded files. Works with search indexes from `sdk.search_indexes`.

**Rephraser:** `sdk.tools.rephraser` — query transformation tool with conversation context for rephrasing.

**Tool configuration on models:**
- `tools` — pass Sequence[FunctionTool] or FunctionTool to model.configure()
- `parallel_tool_calls` — bool, defaults to True
- `tool_choice` — 'none' / 'auto' / 'required' / specific tool dict or FunctionTool

**Tool call result types:**
- AsyncToolCall — .id (str), .function (AsyncFunctionCall)
- AsyncFunctionCall — .name (str), .arguments (JsonObject)

**GitHub example:** `examples/sync/function_calling/assistants/sync_raw_tool_calls_processing.py`

title: YC SDK: image generation, audio/speech, embeddings, tokenization APIs
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026). Capabilities outside of text completions:

**Image Generation (YandexART):**
- Domain: `sdk.models.image_generation('yandex-art')`
- Model config: `.configure(seed=int, width_ratio=1, height_ratio=1, mime_type="image/png")`
- Execution: `run_deferred()` only (async-only), plus `attach_deferred()`
- Context limit: 500 characters. API key scope: `yc.ai.imageGeneration.execute`

**Image Analysis / Vision:**
- No dedicated Vision API in the SDK
- Vision goes through multimodal completions models (Gemma 3 27B supports Base64-encoded images, 256 tokens per image, auto-scaled to 896px longer side)
- Vision OCR is a separate Yandex Cloud service, not part of AI Studio SDK

**Audio / Speech (SpeechKit domain):**
- Domain: `sdk.speechkit` (aliases: `.tts`, `.stt`)
- `text_to_speech` / `tts` → AsyncTextToSpeechFunction
- `speech_to_text` / `stt` → AsyncSpeechToTextFunction
- AudioFormat types: PCM16(sample_rate_hertz, channels=1), MP3, WAV, OGG_OPUS
- STT result types: Utterance (per-utterance), SpeechToTextResult (overall), SpeechToTextStreamingEvent (streaming)
- Realtime API model: `gpt://<folder>/speech-realtime-250923` — combined speech-in/speech-out with LLM

**Embeddings:**
- Domain: `sdk.models.text_embeddings("text-embedding")`
- Model: `.configure(dimensions=256)`, `.run(text, timeout=60, dimensions=Undefined)`
- Fine-tuning: `.tune_deferred(train_datasets, embeddings_tune_type='pair'|'triplet', ...)` and `.tune(poll_timeout=259200, poll_interval=60)`
- Result type: TextEmbeddingsModelResult

**Tokenization:**
- Built-in: `model.tokenize(messages, timeout=60)` → tuple[Token, ...]
- Token usage tracking: `result.usage.input_text_tokens`, `.completion_tokens`, `.total_tokens`

title: YC SDK: assistants, batch, datasets, search, classifiers domains
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026). Additional SDK domains beyond completions:

**Assistants API (full agent framework):**
- `sdk.assistants` — create/manage AI assistants
- `sdk.threads` — conversation threads
- `sdk.runs` — execute runs: .listen(), .submit_tool_results(), .cancel(), .wait(), .get_result(), .get_status()
- `sdk.files` — upload documents (TXT, MD, PDF, DOCX; max 128MB; limit 10k files)
- `sdk.search_indexes` — build search indexes over files (text, vector, hybrid; async construction)
- Run overrides: custom_temperature, custom_max_tokens, custom_max_prompt_tokens, custom_prompt_truncation_options, custom_response_format
- NOTE: AsyncFiles/AsyncThreads/AsyncRuns are marked DEPRECATED in favor of Assistants API

**Batch Processing:**
- `sdk.batch.get()`, `.list_operations()`, `.list_info()`
- AsyncBatchTaskOperation: .delete(), .get_task_info(), .cancel(), .get_result(), .get_status(), .wait()

**Datasets:**
- Types: completions, text_classifiers_binary/multiclass/multilabel, text_embeddings_pair/triplet
- `sdk.datasets.draft_from_path(path)` → AsyncDatasetDraft (.configure(), .upload(), .upload_deferred(), .validate())
- AsyncDataset: .update(), .delete(), .download(), .read(), .list_upload_formats()

**Search API:**
- `sdk.search_api.generative` — generative search with AI-summarized answers + sources
- `sdk.search_api.web` — paginated web search
- `sdk.search_api.image` — image search
- `sdk.search_api.by_image` — reverse image search

**Text Classifiers:**
- `sdk.models.text_classifiers` — zero-shot and few-shot YandexGPT-based classifiers

**Fine-Tuning (sdk.tuning):**
- `sdk.tuning.get(task_id)`, `.list()`
- Available on completions and embeddings models
- Tuning params: seed, lr, n_samples, additional_arguments, tuning_type, scheduler, optimizer
- Completions tuning uses BaseTuningType, BaseScheduler, BaseOptimizer classes
- Default polling: poll_timeout=259200 (72h), poll_interval=60

title: YC SDK: OpenAI-compatible chat domain (sdk.chat.completions)
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026). The `sdk.chat` domain provides an OpenAI-compatible HTTP API:

**Sub-domains:**
- `sdk.chat.completions` → AsyncChatCompletions
- `sdk.chat.text_embeddings` → AsyncChatEmbeddings

**OpenAI-compatible endpoint:** `https://ai.api.cloud.yandex.net/v1` — used by SourceCraft Code Assistant, Roo Code, and any OpenAI SDK client.

This HTTP API supports `extra_query` (arbitrary additional parameters via QueryType | None) which the gRPC completions API does NOT support. All models marked "OpenAI-compatible only" in the model gallery are only accessible through this domain, not through `sdk.models.completions`. These include: DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3 235B, gpt-oss-120b, gpt-oss-20b, Qwen3.5 35B, Gemma 3 27B.

title: YC AI Studio SDK research: auth, installation, LangChain integration
content:
From Yandex AI Studio SDK documentation research (May 31, 2026).

**Installation:** `pip install yandex-ai-studio-sdk`

**Main entry points:** `AIStudio` (sync) / `AsyncAIStudio` (async) — construct with folder_id and auth.

**Auth priority from env vars (auto-detected):**
1. YC_API_KEY — API key
2. YC_IAM_TOKEN — IAM token (max 12h lifetime)
3. YC_OAUTH_TOKEN — OAuth token
4. VM metadata auth (service account on Compute Cloud VM)
5. YC_TOKEN — IAM token (re-read each request)
6. Yandex Cloud CLI profile

**Explicit auth classes:** APIKeyAuth, IAMTokenAuth, OAuthTokenAuth, EnvIAMTokenAuth, MetadataAuth, YandexCloudCLIAuth, NoAuth

**Constructor kwargs:** folder_id, endpoint, auth, retry_policy, yc_profile, service_map, interceptors, enable_server_data_logging, verify (SSL certs)

**LangChain integration:** `model.langchain(model_type='chat', timeout=60)` → BaseYandexLanguageModel compatible with LangChain

**Key limitations:**
- IAM tokens expire in 12h — use EnvIAMTokenAuth or YC_TOKEN env var for auto-refresh
- Common instance models share resources — response time may vary under load
- Logging disabled via enable_server_data_logging=False or x-data-logging-enabled header

title: YC SDK: UNDEFINED sentinel pattern used instead of None for optional params
content:
The `yandex_ai_studio_sdk` v0.20.2 uses a custom `UNDEFINED` sentinel (from `_types/misc.py`) throughout all `configure()`, `__call__()`, and domain methods, not `None`. This distinguishes "parameter not passed" from "explicitly set to None".

```python
class Undefined:
    """Sentinel until PEP 0661."""

UNDEFINED = Undefined()
UndefinedOr = Union[_T, Undefined]
```

Type alias used everywhere: `UndefinedOr[T]` = `Union[T, Undefined]`. All constructor and `configure()` params default to `UNDEFINED`, not `None`. Helper functions: `is_defined(obj)` (TypeGuard), `get_defined_value(obj, default)`.

This means when calling model.configure(...), you must import UNDEFINED if you want to detect omission vs None. The SDK's own configure() uses `UNDEFINED` as default for all params; you pass a value to override, or omit the param to keep current config.

title: YC SDK: Chat Completions vs gRPC Completions key differences (ChatReasoningMode, extra_query, no deferred/tokenize/tune)
content:
The Chat Completions domain (`sdk.chat.completions(...)`, HTTP-based OpenAI-compatible API) differs from the gRPC Completions domain (`sdk.models.completions(...)`) in several significant ways:

1. **Reasoning Mode**: Chat uses `ChatReasoningMode(str, Enum)` with values `'low'`, `'medium'`, `'high'` (maps to `reasoning_effort` in JSON body). gRPC uses `ReasoningMode` with values `DISABLED`, `ENABLED_HIDDEN`.

2. **extra_query param**: Chat model's `configure()` accepts `extra_query: UndefinedOr[QueryType]` — a `dict[str, Any]` of arbitrary additional fields merged into the JSON request body. No equivalent in gRPC models.

3. **No run_deferred()**: Chat models have no `run_deferred()` method. Only gRPC Completions support deferred/async operations.

4. **No tokenize()**: Chat models have no `tokenize()` method.

5. **No tune/tune_deferred()**: Chat models have no tuning methods.

6. **No attachment of deferred operations**: No `attach_deferred()` on Chat models.

7. **Chat models have list()**: `model.list(timeout=60, filters=ModelFilter | None)` to fetch available models from the server via `GET /models`.

8. **Method signatures**: `run(self, messages, *, timeout=180)` and `run_stream(self, messages, *, timeout=180)` — same names but `ChatMessageInputType` instead of `MessageInputType`.

Source: `venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yandex_ai_studio_sdk/_chat/completions/model.py` and `config.py`.

title: YC SDK v0.20.2: Method availability matrix for all model/speech/search domains
content:
From code analysis of `yandex_ai_studio_sdk` v0.20.2 installed at `venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yandex_ai_studio_sdk/`:

Model methods availability matrix:

| Model Type | run() | run_stream() | run_deferred() | configure() | tokenize() | tune() | tune_deferred() | list() | attach_deferred() |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Completions (gRPC) | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | Yes |
| Image Generation | No | No | Yes | Yes | No | No | No | No | No |
| Text Embeddings (gRPC) | Yes | No | No | Yes | No | Yes | Yes | No | No |
| Text Classifiers (gRPC) | Yes | No | No | Yes | No | Yes | Yes | No | No |
| Chat Completions | Yes | Yes | No | Yes | No | No | No | Yes | No |
| Chat Embeddings | Yes | No | No | Yes | No | No | No | Yes | No |
| TTS | Yes | Yes | No | Yes | No | No | No | No | No |
| STT | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | No | No | No | Yes |
| Generative Search | Yes | No | No | Yes | No | No | No | No | No |
| Web Search | Yes | No | No | Yes (via config) | No | No | No | No | No |
| Image Search | Yes | No | No | Yes (via config) | No | No | No | No | No |
| By-Image Search | Yes | No | No | Yes (via config) | No | No | No | No | No |

Also notable: `sdk.speechkit.text_to_speech(...)` and `sdk.speechkit.speech_to_text(...)` have `create_bistream(*, timeout=600)` for bidirectional streaming (returns TTSBidirectionalStream / STTBidirectionalStream).

Source: Code analysis of `_models/`, `_chat/`, `_speechkit/`, `_search_api/` directories.

title: YC SDK: Auth class constructors, env var fallbacks, and auto-detection order
content:
From code analysis of `yandex_ai_studio_sdk` v0.20.2 (`_auth.py`):

All auth classes and their constructors:

- `NoAuth()` — returns None for auth metadata
- `APIKeyAuth(api_key: str)` — header `Api-Key {key}`, env var `YC_API_KEY`
- `IAMTokenAuth(token: str)` — header `Bearer {token}`, env var `YC_IAM_TOKEN`
- `EnvIAMTokenAuth(env_var_name: str | None = None)` — re-reads env on each call, default `YC_TOKEN`
- `OAuthTokenAuth(token: str)` — exchanges OAuth for IAM token via API, emits UserWarning, env `YC_OAUTH_TOKEN`
- `YandexCloudCLIAuth(token: str | None = None, endpoint: str | None = None, yc_profile: str | None = None)` — runs `yc iam create-token`, env `YC_PROFILE`
- `MetadataAuth(token: str | None = None, metadata_url: str | None = None)` — uses `169.254.169.254` metadata endpoint, env `YC_METADATA_ADDR`

Auto-detection order (when no `auth=` param passed to SDK constructor): APIKeyAuth → IAMTokenAuth → OAuthTokenAuth → MetadataAuth → EnvIAMTokenAuth → YandexCloudCLIAuth. Each tries `applicable_from_env()`.

Source: `venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yandex_ai_studio_sdk/_auth.py`.

title: YC SDK: AIStudio/AsyncAIStudio constructor params, Token dataclass, model URI pattern, AsyncOperation defaults
content:
From code analysis of `yandex_ai_studio_sdk` v0.20.2:

**BaseSDK constructor** (both AIStudio and AsyncAIStudio):

```python
def __init__(
    self,
    *,
    folder_id: UndefinedOr[str] | None = UNDEFINED,      # Falls back to YC_FOLDER_ID env var
    endpoint: UndefinedOr[str] | None = UNDEFINED,        # Falls back to YC_API_ENDPOINT env var, or 'api.cloud.yandex.net:443'
    auth: UndefinedOr[str | BaseAuth] = UNDEFINED,        # Falls back to env vars (see auth auto-detection)
    retry_policy: UndefinedOr[RetryPolicy] = UNDEFINED,   # Defaults to RetryPolicy() — 5 attempts, 1s initial backoff, 10s max, 1.5x multiplier, 1s jitter
    yc_profile: UndefinedOr[str] = UNDEFINED,             # YC CLI profile name
    service_map: UndefinedOr[dict[str, str]] = UNDEFINED, # Override endpoints for specific services
    interceptors: UndefinedOr[Sequence[aio.ClientInterceptor]] = UNDEFINED,
    enable_server_data_logging: UndefinedOr[bool] = UNDEFINED,  # Sends x-data-logging-enabled header
    verify: UndefinedOr[bool | PathLike] = UNDEFINED,     # SSL verify: True (default CA), False (disable), or path to CA bundle
)
```

**Token dataclass** (`_models/completions/token.py`):
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Token:
    id: int
    special: bool
    text: str
```

**AsyncOperation defaults**: Default poll timeout = 3600s (1 hour), default poll interval = 10s. Defined in `_types/operation.py`.

**Model URI construction** (`_sdk.py:243`):
```python
def _get_model_uri(self, prefix: Literal['gpt', 'art', 'emb', 'cls'], model_name: str, model_version: str, well_known_names: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> str:
    if '://' in model_name:
        return model_name  # pass through raw URI
    well_known_names = well_known_names or {}
    model_name = well_known_names.get(model_name, model_name)
    return f'{prefix}://{self._folder_id}/{model_name}/{model_version}'
```

URI prefixes: `gpt://` for completions, `art://` for image gen, `emb://` for embeddings, `cls://` for classifiers.

If the model_name already contains `://`, it is used as-is (raw URI passthrough).

**No built-in model name constants**: The SDK v0.20.2 does NOT ship any constants for model names, context sizes, or max tokens. The only hardcoded well-known names are for text embeddings: `'doc'`→`'text-search-doc'`, `'query'`→`'text-search-query'`. Available models must be discovered via `list()` on Chat models or from external documentation.

title: YC SDK _generateStructured docstring references non-existent file: docs/plans/lib-ai-structured-output.md
content:
The `YcAIModel._generateStructured()` docstring at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py:~342` references `docs/plans/lib-ai-structured-output.md §3.6` for the planned implementation. That file does not exist at `docs/plans/`.

The canonical analysis and planning document for YC SDK structured output / tool calling refactoring is `docs/other/yc-ai-sdk/gap-analysis.md`. The reference in the docstring should be updated to point to the correct file, or removed entirely since the gap analysis is the authoritative planning resource.

Note: `docs/plans/` files are historical design documents intentionally left as stale artifacts (per project convention, memory 5dd59667). So the docstring was likely never updated after the restructuring.

title: Yandex AI Studio SDK — model listing on chat domain only
content:
The Yandex Cloud AI Studio SDK provides model listing/discovery only on the OpenAI-compatible HTTP chat domain (`sdk.chat.completions` and `sdk.chat.text_embeddings`). The gRPC-based `sdk.models.completions` domain has NO model listing capability.

**Method**: `.list(timeout=60, filters=None)` on `sdk.chat.completions` or `sdk.chat.text_embeddings`.

**How it works** (live API call, not static list):
- Calls `GET https://llm.api.cloud.yandex.net/v1/models` with header `OpenAI-Project: <folder_id>`.
- The JSON `data` array is filtered to entries whose `id` starts with a prefix (`gpt://` for completions, `emb://` for embeddings).
- Each raw entry has `id` (model URI like `gpt://b1g.../yandexgpt/latest`) and `owned_by` (e.g. `yandex`).

**Returned model objects** have only these properties parsed from the URI:
- `uri` — raw `id` from API
- `owner` — `owned_by` from API
- `name` — parsed from URI (between 2nd and 3rd `/`)
- `version` — parsed from URI (after 3rd `/`, before `@`)
- `fine_tuned` — parsed from URI (whether `@<id>` suffix exists)
- `config` — fresh empty config object

**No context length, capabilities, or other metadata** is extracted by the SDK (even if the raw API returns more fields — the code only reads `id` and `owned_by`).

**Filtering**: via `ModelFilter` TypedDict with optional keys `name`, `owner`, `version`, `fine_tuned`.

title: YC AI SDK doc: model.list() called on wrong object
content:
The documentation at `docs/other/yc-ai-sdk/chat-openai-compat.md` line 111 shows an incorrect code example for model listing:

```python
models = await model.list(timeout=60, filters=None)  # WRONG
```

The correct usage is:

```python
models = await sdk.chat.completions.list(timeout=60, filters=None)  # CORRECT
```

The `list()` method lives on the domain function (`ChatCompletions`/`AsyncChatCompletions`), not on a model instance. This should be corrected when the doc is next touched.

title: YC SDK tool result message format gap — ModelMessage lacks `name` field
content:
The YC SDK expects tool result messages in the format `{"role": "tool", "name": "...", "content": "..."}` where `name` identifies the function that produced the result. However, `ModelMessage` (from `lib/ai/models.py`) only has a `toolCallId` field — it has no `name` field.

This is a design gap documented in `docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md` §9 as an open question. During implementation, either:
- `ModelMessage` needs a `name` field added, or
- A conversion step is needed to map `toolCallId` → function `name` before sending to the SDK

The YC SDK's internal protobuf representation (`_ModelMessageWToolCalls`) likely requires the `name` to match the function tool's registered name. This must be resolved before tool calling with follow-up tool calls works correctly.

title: Benchmark gate required before shipping run_deferred for text generation
content:
The YC SDK refactoring plan (`docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md`) mandates a benchmark comparison before shipping `run_deferred` + `await operation.wait()` for text generation in production. The concern is that deferred operations may add latency overhead compared to direct `model.run()`.

The plan specifies: measure latency of `run_deferred` + `wait()` vs `model.run()` for text generation. If overhead is significant (more than ~10-15%), consider keeping `run()` for text and only using `run_deferred` for image generation where it's the only option.

This is an operational validation criterion, not an implementation detail — the pattern itself is documented in memory 81232025.

title: YC SDK model listing via raw HTTP — endpoint and data gap
content:
The YC SDK refactoring plan (`docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md` §3, §4.5) documents that model listing must use the raw HTTP endpoint because the gRPC domain has no listing capability:

- **Endpoint**: `GET https://llm.api.cloud.yandex.net/v1/models` with `OpenAI-Project: <folder_id>` header
- **Data source**: The HTTP response contains more fields than the SDK's `sdk.chat.completions.list()` wrapper exposes — specifically `id`, `owned_by`, and likely context length, capability flags, and pricing tier information
- **Strategy**: Make the HTTP call directly using the SDK's internal `httpx` client (already authenticated via SDK setup) to capture the full raw response, rather than relying on the SDK's parsed wrapper types
- **Model objects from SDK**: Each model has `uri` (str, e.g. "gpt://folder/yandexgpt/latest"), `owner` (str, e.g. "yandex"), `name` (parsed from URI), `version` (parsed), `fine_tuned` (bool), `config` (fresh empty ChatModelConfig)

Memory a37da157 covers that listing is chat-domain-only; this adds the raw endpoint and "response has more data than SDK exposes" detail.

title: Six-phase implementation plan at docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md
content:
A comprehensive implementation plan was created at `docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md` covering the refactoring of `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`. The plan defines 6 ordered phases:

1. **Per-request model creation (foundation)**: Remove `_initModel()` / `self._ycModel`, add `_getModel(**configOverrides)` factory, update `_generateText`/`_generateImage` to use it + `run_deferred`
2. **Structured output**: Implement `_generateStructured` using `response_format` with JSON Schema
3. **Tool calling**: Add `LLMAbstractTool` → `sdk.tools.function()` conversion, pass tools to `_getModel(tools=...)`, extract tool calls from results
4. **Auth improvements**: Support `auth_type` config with `APIKeyAuth` (primary), `IAMTokenAuth`, `YandexCloudCLIAuth` (fallback); auto-detect from env vars
5. **Model listing**: Add `listRemoteModels()` via HTTP call to `GET https://llm.api.cloud.yandex.net/v1/models`
6. **Tokenization & error handling**: Fix `tokenize()`/`getEstimateTokensCount()`, catch `AIStudioError` hierarchy, map to `ModelResultStatus`

Each phase ends with `make format lint test`. The plan is 10 sections with decision matrices, architecture changes, tool/message format mappings, testing strategy, and acceptance criteria.

title: YC SDK types moved to TYPE_CHECKING after removing _initModel
content:
After the Phase 1 refactoring (removing `self._ycModel` and `_initModel()`), `AsyncGPTModel` and `AsyncImageGenerationModel` are no longer used at runtime — no `isinstance` checks remain, and `self._ycModel`'s type annotation was removed. However, they are still needed for the `_getModel()` return type annotation.

The project pattern: move them to a `TYPE_CHECKING` block so pyright resolves the string-quoted return type hint, but they aren't imported at runtime. Keep `ImageGenerationModelResult` and `AsyncOperation` as regular imports because they are still used at runtime (`.image_bytes`, `operation.wait()`).

```python
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from yandex_ai_studio_sdk._models.completions.model import AsyncGPTModel
    from yandex_ai_studio_sdk._models.image_generation.model import AsyncImageGenerationModel
```

Note: `AsyncOperation` was also removed from imports entirely (not moved to TYPE_CHECKING) because the explicit type annotation on the `operation` variable in `_generateImage` was removed — the cascade is: remove explicit `AsyncOperation[ImageGenerationModelResult]` annotation on `operation` variable → `AsyncOperation` import becomes unused → remove it.

title: YC SDK _getModel union return type requires pyright ignore on attribute access
content:
`_getModel()` returns `AsyncGPTModel | AsyncImageGenerationModel`. When `operation.wait()` is called on the result, pyright sees the return type as `ImageGenerationModelResult | GPTModelResult[AsyncToolCall]` and flags attribute access that only exists on one branch:

- `result.usage` — only on `GPTModelResult`, not on `ImageGenerationModelResult`
- `result.status` — same issue
- `result.alternatives` — same issue
- `result.image_bytes` — only on `ImageGenerationModelResult`

Runtime safety is guaranteed by the `self.supportText` / `self.supportImages` guards checked before calling `_getModel()`, but pyright cannot track this correlation. The fix is `# pyright: ignore[reportAttributeAccessIssue]` on each affected line in `_generateText`, and `# pyright: ignore[reportAssignmentType]` where the union result is assigned to `Optional[ImageGenerationModelResult]` in `_generateImage`. Use `hasattr` guards for `.usage` access (still needs ignore because pyright can't narrow the union via hasattr in all cases with the SDK types).

title: YC SDK: SpeechKit TTS/STT detailed params, input types, and AudioFormat enums
content:
From code analysis of `yandex_ai_studio_sdk` v0.20.2 (`_speechkit/`):

**TTS (`sdk.speechkit.text_to_speech`) configure/__call__ params**:
- `loudness_normalization`: `LoudnessNormalization` (MAX_PEAK, LUFS)
- `audio_format`: `AudioFormat` (MP3, WAV, OGG_OPUS) or `AudioFormat.PCM16(sample_rate_hertz, channels=1)` — classmethod that returns PCM16 with custom config
- `model`: str
- `voice`: str
- `role`: str
- `speed`: float
- `volume`: float
- `pitch_shift`: float
- `duration_ms`: int
- `duration_min_ms`: int
- `duration_max_ms`: int
- `single_chunk_mode`: bool

Methods: `run(text: str) → TextToSpeechResult`, `run_stream(text: str) → AsyncIterator[TextToSpeechResult]`, `create_bistream() → TTSBidirectionalStream`.

**STT (`sdk.speechkit.speech_to_text`) configure/__call__ params**:
- `audio_format`: `AudioFormat` — REQUIRED
- `model`: str
- `language_codes`: LanguageCode (auto, de_DE, en_US, es_ES, fi_FI, fr_FR, he_IL, it_IT, kk_KZ, nl_NL, pl_PL, pt_PT, pt_BR, ru_RU, sv_SE, tr_TR, uz_UZ)
- `text_normalization`: TextNormalization | bool
- `eou_classifier`: EndOfUtteranceClassifier | bool
- `recognition_classifiers`: RecognitionClassifiersInputType | bool
- `speech_analysis`: SpeechAnalysis
- `speaker_labeling`: bool
- `llm_post_process`: LLMPostProcessing

Input types:
- `STTInputType = Union[bytes, Sequence[Union[bytes, int]]]` — raw bytes or mixed bytes (audio chunks) and ints (silence duration in ms)
- `DeferredSTTInputType = Union[str, bytes]` — str = S3 URI, bytes = audio data for upload

Methods: `run(input) → SpeechToTextResult`, `run_stream(input) → AsyncIterator[SpeechToTextStreamingEvent]`, `run_deferred(input) → AsyncOperation[AsyncDeferredSpeechToTextResult]`, `attach_deferred(operation_id)`, `create_bistream() → STTBidirectionalStream`.

Source: `_speechkit/text_to_speech/function.py`, `_speechkit/text_to_speech/tts.py`, `_speechkit/speech_to_text/function.py`, `_speechkit/speech_to_text/stt.py`, `_speechkit/enums.py`.

title: YC SDK v0.20.2: Config dataclass fields for all model types
content:
From code analysis of yandex_ai_studio_sdk v0.20.2, config dataclass fields for each model type:

GPTModelConfig (gRPC completions): temperature, max_tokens, reasoning_mode (DISABLED/ENABLED_HIDDEN), response_format, tools, parallel_tool_calls, tool_choice

ChatModelConfig (HTTP chat completions, extends GPTModelConfig): Same as GPTModelConfig but reasoning_mode uses ChatReasoningMode (low/medium/high maps to reasoning_effort in JSON), plus extra_query (dict[str, Any] for arbitrary JSON body fields)

ImageGenerationModelConfig: seed, width_ratio, height_ratio, mime_type

TextEmbeddingsModelConfig: dimensions

TextClassifiersModelConfig: task_description, labels, samples (few-shot)

TextToSpeechConfig: loudness_normalization, audio_format, model, voice, role, speed, volume, pitch_shift, duration_ms, duration_min_ms, duration_max_ms, single_chunk_mode

SpeechToTextConfig: audio_format (REQUIRED), model, language_codes, text_normalization, eou_classifier, recognition_classifiers, speech_analysis, speaker_labeling, llm_post_process

GenerativeSearchConfig: site, host, url (mutually exclusive), fix_misspell, enable_nrfm_docs, search_filters

WebSearchConfig: search_type (RU/TR/COM/KK/BE/UZ), family_mode, fix_typo_mode, localization, sort_order, sort_mode, group_mode, groups_on_page, docs_in_group, max_passages, region, user_agent, metadata

ImageSearchConfig: search_type, family_mode, fix_typo_mode, format, size, orientation, color, site, docs_on_page, user_agent

Source: Code analysis of _models/completions/config.py, _chat/completions/config.py, _models/image_generation/model.py, _models/text_embeddings/model.py, _models/text_classifiers/model.py, _speechkit/ config files, _search_api/ config files.

title: YC SDK package installed under python3.13 not python3.12 in venv
content:
The installed `yandex_ai_studio_sdk` package (v0.20.2) lives at `venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yandex_ai_studio_sdk/`, not `venv/lib/python3.12/`. The project uses Python 3.13 in its venv despite AGENTS.md saying "Python 3.12 or newer". This matters when reading SDK source code or importing SDK internals in scripts.

Full install path: `/Users/vgoshev/Development/NotA/gromozeka/venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages/yandex_ai_studio_sdk/`

title: Bug: YC SDK _generateText unhandled JSONDecodeError in tool-call argument parsing
Bug: YC SDK _convertMessages treats toolCallId as function name, but LLM service stores call ID
content:
In `YcAIModel._convertMessages()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py:224-226`, the code treats `ModelMessage.toolCallId` as the function name for YC SDK tool result messages:

```python
if m.toolCallId is not None:
    # toolCallId stores the function name per design decision
    result.append({"name": m.toolCallId, "content": m.content})
```

However, the LLM service at `internal/services/llm/service.py:369` sets `toolCallId=toolCall.id` (the LLM's tool call identifier like "call_123"), NOT the function name. This means `_convertMessages` sends the wrong value in the `"name"` field, which the YC SDK requires to be the registered function name (e.g., "get_weather").

This goes beyond the previously-known design gap (ModelMessage lacks a `name` field) — it's a concrete wiring bug where the actual data flow violates the conversion layer's assumption.

Fix options (pick one):
- **Option A (recommended)**: Have `_convertMessages` look up the function name from the preceding assistant message's `toolCalls` by matching `toolCallId` to the tool call ID.
- **Option B**: Add a `toolName` field to `ModelMessage` and populate it in the LLM service alongside `toolCallId`.

No fix should break the OpenAI provider, which uses `tool_call_id` for the same field.

title: YC SDK _convertMessages sends OpenAI-format tool_calls for assistant messages — likely incompatible
content:
In `YcAIModel._convertMessages()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py:228`, assistant messages with tool calls (`m.toolCallId is None` but `m.toolCalls` is set) are serialized via `m.toDict("text")`, which produces OpenAI-format `tool_calls` dicts: `{"id": "...", "function": {"name": "...", "arguments": "..."}, "type": "function"}`.

The YC SDK's gRPC-based message format likely expects either its own protobuf-derived dict structure for tool calls, or no `tool_calls` field at all (as suggested in the plan doc §6.2 which shows `{"role": "assistant", "text": result.text}` without tool_calls). Passing OpenAI-format tool_calls may cause the SDK to reject the message or silently ignore the tool calls, breaking multi-turn tool-calling conversations.

Fix: strip the `tool_calls` key from assistant message dicts before passing to the SDK:
```python
if m.toolCalls:
    result.append({"role": m.role, "text": m.content})
else:
    result.append(m.toDict("text"))
```

This should be validated with a live API call as flagged in `docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md` §9.2.

title: YC SDK error handling is inconsistent across _generateText, _generateImage, _generateStructured
content:
In `YcAIModel` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, the three generation methods handle errors inconsistently:

- `_generateText` catches only `AIStudioError` → returns error via `_handleSDKError`
- `_generateImage` catches `Exception` (too broad) → returns error via `_handleSDKError` (swallows bugs like `AttributeError`, `KeyError`)
- `_generateStructured` catches `Exception` → re-raises (never returns error results for API errors; base class catches but doesn't get proper status mapping)

This inconsistency means:
1. `_generateStructured` never maps API errors (content filter, rate limit) to `ModelStructuredResult(status=CONTENT_FILTER)` — they become unhandled exceptions
2. `_generateImage` silently swallows programming errors as model errors

Fix: make all three consistent by catching only `AIStudioError` in the `try/except`, moving non-SDK error handling (like JSON decode) closer to the specific operation that can fail.

title: YC SDK _generateStructured docstring references non-existent validation — support_text/support_images mismatch
content:
In `YcAIModel.__init__()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py:121`, the docstring says `Raises: ValueError: If both support_text and support_images are True or both are False`. However, the old `_initModel()` method that enforced this validation was removed in the refactoring. The new `_getModel()` does NOT perform this validation — having both `True` silently takes the text branch, and both `False` raises an error only at generation time.

Fix: either re-add the validation in `__init__` or update the docstring to reflect deferred validation.

title: YC SDK _statusToModelRunResultStatus raises ValueError on unknown status codes
YC SDK getExactTokensCount passes raw data without format conversion
content:
In `YcAIModel.getExactTokensCount()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py:486-504`, the method accepts `data: Any` and passes it directly to `model.tokenize(data)`. The SDK tokenizer may expect a specific format (e.g., list of SDK `TextMessage` objects, or a plain string). If `data` is a list of `ModelMessage` objects, this will likely fail because the SDK won't understand our internal message format.

The base class `getEstimateTokensCount` handles both `str` and arbitrary objects via `json.dumps`, so `getExactTokensCount` should be at least as robust. Either convert `ModelMessage` lists via `_convertMessages()` first, or document what format `data` should be in.

title: YC SDK provider refactoring completed — all 6 phases implemented
content:
The six-phase refactoring of `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py` (planned in `docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md`) is now fully implemented and all 13 acceptance criteria are marked completed. Key accomplishments per phase:

- **Phase 1 (Per-request model creation)**: `_initModel()`/`self._ycModel` removed, `_getModel()` creates fresh SDK model per request, eliminating `.configure()` mutation race conditions. All generation uses `run_deferred()` + `await operation.wait()` streaming.
- **Phase 2 (Structured output)**: `_generateStructured()` now implemented via `response_format` parameter on the SDK model. No longer raises `NotImplementedError`.
- **Phase 3 (Tool/function calling)**: `_convertTools()`, `_convertMessages()`, and `tool_calls` extraction from SDK response all implemented.
- **Phase 4 (Auth)**: `_resolveAuth()` supports `api_key` (APIKeyAuth), `iam_token` (IAMTokenAuth), `yc_cli` (YandexCloudCLIAuth), `auto` (AutoAuth).
- **Phase 5 (Error handling)**: `_handleSDKError()` centralized error handler for all SDK exceptions.
- **Phase 6 (Model listing & tokenization)**: `getExactTokensCount()` for accurate token counting via SDK; `listModels()` for model enumeration.

Related docs updated: `docs/llm/libraries.md`, `docs/other/yc-ai-sdk/gap-analysis.md`, `docs/plans/yc-sdk-provider-refactoring.md`, `docs/llm/configuration.md`, `docs/llm/index.md`, `docs/developer-guide.md`.

title: Stale documentation: libraries.md claimed yc-sdk _generateStructured raises NotImplementedError
content:
After the YC SDK provider refactoring was completed, `docs/llm/libraries.md` had a stale claim at lines 162-164 stating that the `yc-sdk` provider's `_generateStructured` raises `NotImplementedError` (linking to `docs/plans/lib-ai-structured-output.md` for explanation). This was a remnant from Phase 2 planning and was no longer accurate — the refactoring implemented structured output via `response_format`.

The stale section was updated to describe the actual implementation: per-request model creation (`_getModel()`), structured output via `response_format`, tool calling support, auth methods, tokenization (`getExactTokensCount()`), and centralized error handling (`_handleSDKError()`).

Lesson: When a plan-to-implement doc says something "will raise NotImplementedError for now", check whether the implementation has since been completed and update the stale reference.

title: Bug: YC SDK _generateText for loop over toolCallsRaw outside if guard — iterates None
content:
In `YcAIModel._generateText()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, there was a structural logic bug found and fixed during the 2026-05-31 code review fixes.

**Root cause**: The `if toolCallsRaw:` guard (which checks whether the SDK result has tool calls) only set `resultStatus = ModelResultStatus.TOOL_CALLS`, but the `for call in toolCallsRaw:` loop that processes tool calls was at the **same indentation level** — not inside the `if` block. This meant the `for` loop would execute even when `toolCallsRaw` is `None`, causing `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable`.

**Fix**: Indented the entire `for` loop body (tool call extraction with JSON parsing and `LLMToolCall` construction) inside the `if toolCallsRaw:` block.

**Discovered**: As a side effect of fixing the `json.JSONDecodeError` wrapping (fix #2 of code review). The pre-existing bug was masked because most tool-using requests actually have `toolCallsRaw` non-None, so it rarely triggered in practice.

title: Known drift: YC OpenAI base URL changed (llm.api → ai.api) but tests not updated — 4 pre-existing failures
content:
**Status**: Known pre-existing test failures, NOT caused by YC SDK provider changes.

**Symptom**: 4 tests in `test_yc_openai_provider.py` fail consistently:
1. `test_yc_openai_basic` — golden data test, no matching recorded call
2. `test_yc_openai_structured` — golden data test, no matching recorded call
3. `testYcOpenaiProviderGetBaseUrl` — expects `https://llm.api.cloud.yandex.net/v1` but code returns `https://ai.api.cloud.yandex.net/v1`
4. `testYcOpenaiProviderClientInitialization` — same base URL mismatch

**Root cause**: The YC OpenAI provider code (`YcOpenaiProvider`) was updated to use `ai.api.cloud.yandex.net` (the correct current endpoint), but `test_yc_openai_provider.py` still expects the old `llm.api.cloud.yandex.net` base URL. The golden data files also need regeneration.

**Confirmed**: Failures are pre-existing — verified by running `git stash && make test` on the clean baseline (commit `11fa042`), which showed the exact same 4 failures.

**Impact**: These failures prevent `make test` from passing even on clean master, which interferes with quality gates. They need to be fixed independently of any YC SDK provider work.

title: YC SDK _ModelMessageWToolCalls must construct protobuf ToolCallList — ToolCall proto has no id field
content:
When subclassing the SDK's `TextMessageWithToolCallsProtocol` to pass assistant messages with tool calls (e.g., in `_ModelMessageWToolCalls` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`), the `tool_calls` attribute must be a properly constructed `ToolCallList`.

HOW `message_to_proto()` USES IT:
- The SDK's `message_to_proto()` (in `yandex_ai_studio_sdk/_models/completions/message.py:52-54`) does:
  ```python
  if isinstance(message, TextMessageWithToolCallsProtocol) and message.tool_calls:
      kwargs = {'role': message.role, 'tool_call_list': message.tool_calls._proto_origin}
  ```
- It ONLY reads `message.tool_calls._proto_origin` (the protobuf `ToolCallList`)
- The Python-level `tool_calls` tuple is only used for truthiness check (`__len__`)
- An empty `ToolCallList(tool_calls=())` is falsy — the branch is never taken

CONSTRUCTING THE PROTOBUF OBJECTS:
- Need to build `ProtoCompletionsToolCallList` (from `yandex.cloud.ai.foundation_models.v1.text_common_pb2`) from `LLMToolCall` data
- Each `ProtoCompletionsToolCall` has NO `id` field — IDs only exist in JSON/HTTP layer. The SDK's own `_from_proto` always sets `id=None`.
- `ProtoCompletionsToolCall` only has `function_call` field with `name` (string) and `arguments` (protobuf Struct)
- Build the arguments struct via `google.protobuf.struct_pb2.Struct().update(tc.parameters)`

IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN:
```python
from google.protobuf.struct_pb2 import Struct
from yandex.cloud.ai.foundation_models.v1.text_common_pb2 import (
    FunctionCall as ProtoCompletionsFunctionCall,
    ToolCall as ProtoCompletionsToolCall,
    ToolCallList as ProtoCompletionsToolCallList,
)

protoToolCalls = []
sdkToolCalls = []
for tc in modelMessage.toolCalls:
    argsStruct = Struct()
    if tc.parameters:
        argsStruct.update(tc.parameters)
    protoFc = ProtoCompletionsFunctionCall(name=tc.name, arguments=argsStruct)
    protoTc = ProtoCompletionsToolCall(function_call=protoFc)  # NO id parameter
    protoToolCalls.append(protoTc)
    fc = AsyncFunctionCall(name=tc.name, arguments=tc.parameters or {}, _proto_origin=protoFc)
    sdkToolCalls.append(AsyncToolCall(id=None, function=fc, _proto_origin=protoTc, _json_origin=None))

protoList = ProtoCompletionsToolCallList(tool_calls=protoToolCalls)
self.tool_calls = ToolCallList(tool_calls=tuple(sdkToolCalls), _proto_origin=protoList)

title: Pyright can't resolve constructor kwargs on Type[TypeVar] — use @overload instead
YC SDK protobuf ToolCall has no id field — only available in JSON/HTTP layer
content:
The protobuf `ToolCall` message (from `yandex.cloud.ai.foundation_models.v1.text_common_pb2.ToolCall`) has NO `id` field. It only contains a `function_call` field with `name` (string) and `arguments` (protobuf Struct). Tool call IDs exist only in the JSON/HTTP representation layer.

Evidence: The SDK's own `BaseToolCall._from_proto()` constructs `ToolCall` objects with `id=None`:
```python
return cls(id=None, function=function, _proto_origin=proto, _json_origin=None)
```

When constructing `ProtoCompletionsToolCall` protobuf objects directly (to create `ToolCallList._proto_origin`), do NOT pass an `id` parameter. It's not a valid field on the proto message.

title: Plan agent: restricted to planning only, cannot execute or delegate
content: The "plan" agent (model `yteam:internal/smart`) operates under severe execution constraints: it cannot make file modifications, cannot run commands, cannot use the `task` tool to delegate execution to other agents, and can only produce plans and ask clarifying questions. When a user gives affirmative confirmation (e.g., "yeah", "do it"), the plan agent must respond with a detailed execution plan suitable for handoff to a software-developer or similar agent, rather than attempting to execute itself. This is a distinct behavior from the "teamlead" agent (which can delegate via `task` whitelist) and the "code-analyst" agent (which has `task: deny` but is documented as unable to delegate). The plan agent's constraint appears to be hard-coded at the agent definition level rather than a permission-block configuration.

title: YC SDK _convertMessages builds callIdToName map but never uses it — tool results get wrong name field
content:
In `YcAIModel._convertMessages()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, lines 312-317 build a `callIdToName` dict to resolve function names from tool call IDs found in `_generateText`'s tool call messages. However, the tool result construction on line 343 uses `m.toolCallId` directly as the `name` field: `{"name": m.toolCallId, "content": m.content}`. The `callIdToName` map is never actually consulted.

The YC SDK `tool_results` format expects `{"name": <function_name>, "content": <result>}`, but `ModelMessage.toolCallId` stores the call identifier (e.g. `"call_abc123"`) — set by `internal/services/llm/service.py:369` from `toolCall.id`. So tool call IDs are sent where function names are expected.

Fix: look up the function name from `callIdToName`:
```python
funcName = callIdToName.get(str(m.toolCallId), str(m.toolCallId))
lastToolResult.append({"name": funcName, "content": m.content})
```

This was identified as CRITICAL — tool-call round-trips silently send wrong data to the API.

title: YC SDK result.usage accessed without None guard across three generation methods
content:
In `YcAIModel` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, three generation methods (`_generateText` ~line 497-499, `_generateStructured` ~line 605-607, `_generateImage` ~line 615-617) directly access `result.usage.input_text_tokens`, `result.usage.completion_tokens`, and `result.usage.total_tokens` without checking whether `result.usage` is None.

The previous `_extractUsage` helper (with `hasattr` guards) was removed during refactoring. The YC SDK does not guarantee `usage` will always be populated — gRPC streaming responses, partial errors, or API changes may return `usage=None`, causing `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'input_text_tokens'`.

Fix: either restore a safe extraction helper or add inline guards:
```python
inputTokens = getattr(result.usage, "input_text_tokens", None) if result.usage else None
```

title: YC SDK _ModelMessageWToolCalls: tool call ID falls back to function name — duplicate IDs for parallel calls
content:
In `_ModelMessageWToolCalls` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, line 486, tool call IDs are constructed with:
```python
id=call.id if call.id else call.function.name,
```
When `call.id` is None (the gRPC/protobuf path where IDs may not be provided), the ID falls back to the function name. If the model calls the same function twice in one response (parallel tool calls with different arguments), both `LLMToolCall` objects get the same ID. The caller (`internal/services/llm/service.py`) uses `toolCall.id` to correlate results back to calls, so duplicate IDs would cause results to be associated with the wrong invocation or only one result being recorded.

Fix: generate a UUID when ID is missing: `id=call.id if call.id else str(uuid.uuid4())`. The `uuid` module is already used in the codebase.

title: YC SDK _handleSDKError overload return type technically lies with ModelRunResult
content:
In `YcAIModel._handleSDKError()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, lines 394-432, the implementation declares return type `ModelRunResult` with default `retType: Type[ModelRunResult] = ModelRunResult`. However, the first `@overload` (line 378-384) declares `retType: Type[ModelStructuredResult] -> ModelStructuredResult`. When `retType=ModelStructuredResult` is passed, the runtime call returns `ModelStructuredResult(...)` (a subclass of `ModelRunResult`), which works at runtime but the implementation's return type annotation is technically incorrect.

Pyright in `basic` mode may or may not flag this. `ModelStructuredResult.__init__` has a `data` parameter (defaults to `None`) that `ModelRunResult` doesn't have, but the call `retType(rawResult=None, status=..., resultText=..., error=...)` passes `resultText=errorMsg` which is inherited — so data defaults to None correctly for error cases.

Fix: use a TypeVar bound to ModelRunResult for type-safe overloads:
```python
_R = TypeVar("_R", bound=ModelRunResult)

@overload
def _handleSDKError(self, error: Exception, *, retType: Type[_R]) -> _R: ...
```

title: YC SDK inconsistent exception handling: _generateText catches AIStudioError, _generateImage catches Exception
content:
In `YcAIModel` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, `_generateText` (line 502) catches `AIStudioError`, while `_generateImage` (line 542) catches bare `Exception`. This inconsistency means:
- `_generateText` lets non-SDK exceptions (TypeError, KeyError, etc.) propagate up unhandled
- `_generateImage` swallows all exceptions into a `ModelRunResult` with ERROR status, masking unexpected errors

Fix: decide on a consistent strategy. Catching `AIStudioError` is more precise. If defensive catching is desired, catch `Exception` in both but log a warning when the exception is not an `AIStudioError`.

title: YC SDK uses assert isinstance() for runtime type checks — stripped under -O
content:
In `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, multiple `assert isinstance(...)` statements are used as runtime type guards (e.g., checking `model` is `AsyncGPTModel`, `result` is `GPTModelResult`). Python's `assert` statements are stripped entirely when running with `-O` / `PYTHONOPTIMIZE=1`. If the SDK ever returns an unexpected type (version upgrade, API change), the code will crash with a confusing `AttributeError` deep in the call stack instead of a clear immediate error.

This is the same pattern previously flagged in `internal/services/llm/service.py` (memory #4d8c27f8). Fix: replace assertions with explicit `isinstance` checks raising `TypeError`:
```python
if not isinstance(model, AsyncGPTModel):
    raise TypeError(f"Expected AsyncGPTModel, got {type(model).__name__}")
```

title: YC SDK tool result batching: lastToolResultRole used as truthy guard — empty string breaks flush
content:
In `YcAIModel._convertMessages()` at `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, the batching pattern combines consecutive tool result messages into `{"tool_results": [...]}` dicts. The flush logic on line 323 checks `lastToolResult and lastToolResultRole` — both must be truthy to flush. Since `lastToolResultRole` is set from `m.role`, if a tool result message somehow has an empty string role (falsy), the flush would never trigger and tool results would accumulate silently. The final flush on line 359 has the same issue.

Also, the `lastToolResultRole` is used as a dict key in the output (e.g., `{"role": lastToolResultRole, "tool_results": ...}`). If the role is `"tool"`, the SDK message gets a `"role": "tool"` key alongside `"tool_results"` — need to verify this is what the SDK expects (tool_results messages may not need/expect an explicit role key since the format implies it).

title: Overload ordering: Type[Sub] overload must come before Type[Base] overload
content:
When using `@overload` with `Type[ModelStructuredResult]` and `Type[ModelRunResult]`, the more specific overload (returning `ModelStructuredResult`) MUST be listed FIRST. This is because `Type[X]` is **covariant** in X — `Type[ModelStructuredResult]` is assignable to `Type[ModelRunResult]` — so when pyright matches overloads top-to-bottom, both match a call with `retType=ModelStructuredResult`. If the `ModelRunResult` overload is first, pyright picks it and returns `ModelRunResult`, causing a `reportReturnType` error at the call site.

**Fix:** Order overloads from most-specific to least-specific:

```python
@overload
def _handleSDKError(self, error: Exception, *, retType: Type[ModelStructuredResult]) -> ModelStructuredResult: ...

@overload
def _handleSDKError(self, error: Exception, *, retType: Type[ModelRunResult] = ...) -> ModelRunResult: ...
```

This applies to any overloaded function where one parameter's `Type[Sub]` is a subtype of another's `Type[Base]`.

title: scripts/check_tool_calling.py — diagnostic script for LLM tool-calling probe
content:
Created `scripts/check_tool_calling.py` (patterned after `check_structured_output.py`) to test whether LLM models properly invoke function-calling tools. Key design:

- Defines a `get_current_weather` fake tool with `location` (required) and `unit` (optional) parameters
- Sends a query that should trigger function call ("What's the weather in Minsk?")
- Iterates all text-capable models, force-enables `support_tools` on each
- Implements a full multi-turn tool-calling loop (up to 5 turns) mirroring `LLMService`: when model returns TOOL_CALLS, executes the fake weather function, appends assistant + tool result messages to the conversation, and calls model again
- Classification logic tracks `toolCalledCorrectly` across the entire loop — if model invoked `get_current_weather` with valid params at ANY turn, result is PASS (not just based on last result)
- Exit codes: 0 (all consistent), 1 (some inconsistent), 2 (errors)
- Features: `--verbose` flag shows full conversation history via `ModelMessage.toLogMessage()`, elapsed time per model, summary table with flag-vs-reality consistency markers
- Shell runner at `scripts/run-check-tool-calling.sh` follows standard pattern

title: gRPC fork_posix.cc log suppression via GRPC_VERBOSITY env var
content:
When the YC SDK model (yandex-ai-studio-sdk) is called for the first time, gRPC's C++ core emits a message like:
```
I0514 21:02:59.800020 30097272 fork_posix.cc:71] Other threads are currently calling into gRPC, skipping fork() handlers
```

This comes from `grpcio` (a dependency of `yandex-ai-studio-sdk`), printed via Google glog directly to stderr — NOT controlled by Python's `logging` module. The fix is two-fold:

1. Set `os.environ.setdefault("GRPC_VERBOSITY", "ERROR")` BEFORE any gRPC import (must be at module top, right after `from __future__ import annotations` and before other imports)
2. Also silence Python logger: `logging.getLogger("grpc").setLevel(logging.ERROR)`

In `scripts/check_tool_calling.py`, the env var is set via a special pattern: docstring → `from __future__ import annotations` → `import os; os.environ.setdefault(...)` → remaining stdlib imports (with `# noqa: E402`) → sys.path manipulation → project imports (with `# noqa: E402`). This is because the env var must be set before any import that triggers gRPC loading, but `from __future__` must be the first statement after the docstring.

title: Flake8 E704 fires on @overload stubs with inline ellipsis body
content:
Flake8 rule E704 ("multiple statements on one line") fires on @overload-decorated function stubs when the ellipsis body `...` is on the same line as the def signature. Example that triggers it:
```
@overload
def _handleSDKError(self, error: Exception, *, retType: Type[ModelStructuredResult]) -> ModelStructuredResult: ...
```
Fix: Add `# noqa: E704` inline after the `...`:
```
@overload
def _handleSDKError(self, error: Exception, *, retType: Type[ModelStructuredResult]) -> ModelStructuredResult: ...  # noqa: E704
```
This is the standard pattern for @overload stubs in projects using flake8 with E704 enabled. Note: black auto-formatter will keep the ellipsis on the same line as def, so the inline noqa is the appropriate fix rather than trying to expand to multi-line (which black will collapse back).

title: Bug: tool-call classification only looked at last result, lost successful tool calls from earlier turns
content:
In `scripts/check_tool_calling.py`, the tool-call classification initially classified based on the LAST `ModelRunResult` only. When the model called the tool on turn 1 (TOOL_CALLS) and then returned a text response on turn 2 (FINAL), `classifyResult` saw the FINAL response with no tool calls and returned NO_TOOL_CALL, overwriting the fact that the tool was successfully invoked.

Fix: track `toolCalledCorrectly: bool` across the entire loop. Set it to True whenever the model returns TOOL_CALLS with the correct tool name and valid parameters. The final classification is determined by this boolean, not by the last result alone.

Bug pattern: when evaluating tool-calling support, don't classify the loop-exit result — classify whether the tool was correctly called at ANY point during the conversation.

title: OpenAI SDK models.list() yields Model objects directly, not pages with .data
content:
When calling `self._client.models.list()` in the OpenAI Python SDK, the method returns an `AsyncPager[Model]` or similar async iterable that yields `Model` objects directly — NOT pages with a `.data` attribute.

**Wrong pattern (easily guessed from pagination API experience):**
```python
modelsPage = await self._client.models.list()
async for page in modelsPage:
    for model in page.data:  # ❌ Model has no .data attribute
        ...
```

**Correct pattern:**
```python
models = await self._client.models.list()
async for model in models:  # ✅ Yields Model objects directly
    result[model.id] = model.model_dump()
```

This was caught by pyright during the `listRemoteModels()` implementation in `BasicOpenAIProvider` (lib/ai/providers/basic_openai_provider.py). Pyright error: `Cannot access attribute "data" for class "Model"`.

The same direct-iteration pattern applies across other OpenAI SDK list operations (e.g., `files.list()`, `fine_tuning.jobs.list()`). Always check the SDK's actual return types — `AsyncPaginator`/`AsyncPager` types often yield items directly rather than page objects.

title: YC SDK _ModelMessageWToolCalls: Struct.update() crashes on None values in tool call parameters
content:
In `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, `_ModelMessageWToolCalls.__init__()` calls `argsStruct.update(tc.parameters)` at line ~119, where `argsStruct` is a protobuf `Struct` and `tc.parameters` is a dict from the LLM tool call.

**Bug**: If `tc.parameters` contains keys with `None` values (e.g., `{"location": None, "units": "celsius"}`), protobuf's `Struct.update()` raises `TypeError` because the protobuf `Value` wrapper type has no null scalar type — it only supports `float`, `bool`, `string`, `list`, and `struct`.

**Realistic scenario**: LLMs frequently return `null` for optional function parameters. A tool call like `get_weather(location="Moscow", units=None)` would crash at runtime.

**Fix**: Filter out `None` values from the dict before calling `update()`:

```python
if tc.parameters:
    filtered = {k: v for k, v in tc.parameters.items() if v is not None}
    if filtered:
        argsStruct.update(filtered)
```

Note: The existing `if tc.parameters:` guard at line 118 only skips when `parameters` itself is `None`/falsy — it does not protect against `None` values *inside* the dict. Code review finding from branch `some-lib-ai-refactoring`.

title: YC SDK _handleSDKError: overload signatures correct but implementation return type annotation too narrow
content:
In `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, `_handleSDKError` at line ~397 has `@overload` signatures that correctly declare both `ModelStructuredResult` and `ModelRunResult` as return types. However, the actual implementation signature says `-> ModelRunResult`, which is incorrect when `retType=ModelStructuredResult` is passed.

Since `ModelStructuredResult` extends `ModelRunResult`, this is technically a valid lower bound — pyright won't flag it. But callers expecting `ModelStructuredResult` won't get proper type safety from type checkers.

**Fix**: Change the implementation signature to `-> ModelRunResult | ModelStructuredResult` (or `Union[ModelRunResult, ModelStructuredResult]`).

Code review finding from branch `some-lib-ai-refactoring`.

title: YcAIModel and YcAIProvider use mutable default argument extraConfig={} — shared dict across calls
content:
In `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, both `YcAIModel.__init__()` (line ~168) and `YcAIProvider.addModel()` use `extraConfig: Dict[str, Any] = {}` as a default argument. This is a well-known Python gotcha: the same mutable dict object is shared across all calls that use the default.

While the current code doesn't mutate `extraConfig` after receiving it (it's passed through to `self._config`), this is a risky pattern that could cause surprising bugs if future code mutates the dict in-place. If two models are created with no `extraConfig`, they'd share the same `{}` dict object, and mutating one would affect the other.

**Fix**: Use `extraConfig: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None` and `extraConfig = extraConfig or {}` in the body, consistent with Python best practice.

Code review finding from branch `some-lib-ai-refactoring`.

title: YC SDK _convertMessages omitRole parameter is dead code — no caller passes it
content:
In `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py`, `_convertMessages()` accepts `omitRole: bool = False` as a parameter. However, no caller in the codebase ever passes this parameter with `True`. The entire `omitRole`-guarded code branches (lines ~324-329, 353-354, 359-363) are dead code.

The dead branches handle the case where tool result messages have their `role` field omitted (only `name` and `content`). Since the parameter is never used, this creates confusion for maintainers:

1. The tool result grouping logic is harder to follow because of the conditional branches.
2. Future developers may think `omitRole=True` is a valid mode and add callers that don't work as expected.

**Fix**: Either remove the `omitRole` parameter and its branches entirely, or add a comment documenting where it's intended to be used (with a TODO if it's for future use like Max Messenger compat).

Code review finding from branch `some-lib-ai-refactoring`.

title: YC SDK _getModel: call.function is None guard is unreachable after isinstance check
content:
In `lib/ai/providers/yc_sdk_provider.py` at line ~475-483, inside `_getModel()`, the code iterates tool calls and checks:

```python
if not isinstance(call.function, AsyncFunctionCall):
    logger.error(f"Tool call function is not AsyncFunctionCall: {type(call.function).__name__}")
    continue
if call.function is None:
    logger.error(f"Tool call function is None: {call!r}")
    continue
```

The second guard (`if call.function is None:`) is **unreachable dead code**. If `call.function` were `None`, the `isinstance` check on the previous line would be `False` (since `isinstance(None, AsyncFunctionCall)` is always `False`), and the `continue` at line 479 would execute. Line 481 can never be reached.

**Impact**: No runtime bug, but misleading for maintainers. The dead code suggests a misunderstanding of the control flow — someone may have intended a different order or wanted to add a separate `None` check before the `isinstance` check.

**Fix**: Remove the `if call.function is None` guard. If the intent was to guard against `None` instead of wrong type, restructure as:
```python
if call.function is None:
    logger.error(f"Tool call function is None: {call!r}")
    continue
if not isinstance(call.function, AsyncFunctionCall):
    logger.error(f"Tool call function is not AsyncFunctionCall: {type(call.function).__name__}")
    continue
```

Code review finding from branch `some-lib-ai-refactoring`.

title: sqlToCustomType Union pre-check: avoid conversion when data already matches expected type
content:
In `internal/database/utils.py`, the `sqlToCustomType` function was enhanced to handle `Union` / `Optional` types (like `Optional[int]`, `str | None`) more robustly. Previously, it iterated through all Union member types and attempted conversion on each. The fix (commit `decd2af`) added a two-phase approach:

**Phase 1 (pre-check)**: For each Union member type `arg`, first call `_checkType(data, arg)` to see if the data already matches that type. If it does, call `sqlToCustomType(data, arg)` recursively with only that specific type. If conversion succeeds, return immediately.

**Phase 2 (fallback)**: If Phase 1 didn't find a match (e.g., data is a string that needs parsing into int), fall through to the original logic of trying each member type with conversion.

This prevents:
- Double-processing of already-correctly-typed data
- Edge cases where conversion logic would incorrectly fail on data that's already the right type
- Unnecessary parsing overhead for data that came from SQL already in the expected format

The fix was motivated by real scenarios where `Optional[bool]`, `Optional[int]`, or other Union types with simple types caused failures — the conversion functions would try to process values that were already valid typed objects (from DB read), not raw SQL bytes.

title: /llm_replay dev command architecture: file attachment + model param + full tool context
content:
The `/llm_replay` dev command was designed (plan at `docs/plans/llm-replay-and-yaml-conversion.md`) as a handler in `internal/bot/common/handlers/dev_commands.py` (existing `DevCommandsHandler`) for replaying LLM queries with full bot tool context.

Key design decisions:
- **File source**: JSON file sent as a Telegram document attachment (not a local filesystem path). Downloaded via `self._bot.downloadAttachment()`.
- **Model parameter**: Model name is a **required** command parameter: `/llm_replay <model_name>` with attached JSON file.
- **Tool usage**: Uses all tools registered in `LLMService` (via `generateTextViaLLM`, not `generateText`). The chat's `USE_TOOLS` setting is respected — if disabled for the chat, tools are not passed to the LLM even though they're registered.
- **Output**: Streams intermediate LLM responses (tool calls, thinking) back to the chat, then reports a final summary with token counts and tool call details.
- **Message reconstruction**: Parses the attached JSON into `ModelMessage` list for `LLMService.generateTextViaLLM()`, using the same reconstruction logic as `scripts/run_llm_debug_query.py`.

This differs from the standalone `scripts/run_llm_debug_query.py` (which has no tools) and `scripts/reproduce_llm_dialog.py` (which reads from DB without calling LLM). The dev command runs inside the full bot process with all services initialized.

title: convert_readable_to_llm_log.py: reverse conversion of readable YAML back to JSON log format
content:
A standalone script `scripts/convert_readable_to_llm_log.py` was planned (plan at `docs/plans/llm-replay-and-yaml-conversion.md`) as the inverse of `scripts/convert_llm_log_to_readable.py`. It converts the human-readable YAML format back to the one-JSON-per-line format that `scripts/run_llm_debug_query.py` consumes.

Key details:
- No bot code changes needed — standalone script with no bot dependencies (PyYAML only).
- Reverses the transformations done by `convert_llm_log_to_readable.py`: collapses YAML literal block multiline strings back to `\n`-escaped single lines, strips YAML header comments, outputs one JSON object per line (matching the original LLM log format).
- Supports `--pretty` flag to output a formatted JSON array instead of one-per-line.
- Enables the edit→replay workflow: convert log → YAML → edit in YAML → convert back to JSON → run through `/llm_replay` or `run_llm_debug_query.py`.

title: reconstructMessages utility extraction for LLM message replay
content:
During the `/llm_replay` dev command design, it was identified that `scripts/run_llm_debug_query.py` contains a `reconstructMessages` function that parses a saved LLM interaction JSON into `List[ModelMessage]`. Both the standalone script and the new `/llm_replay` bot command need this same logic.

Decision: Extract `reconstructMessages` into `internal/services/llm/utils.py` as a shared utility so:
1. `scripts/run_llm_debug_query.py` imports it from the service module instead of defining its own.
2. The `/llm_replay` dev command in `DevCommandsHandler` imports the same function.
3. The LLM log JSON format remains the single source of truth for serialized message history.
4. Any future tool or script that needs to rehydrate saved LLM interactions uses the same canonical path.

This avoids duplicating message reconstruction logic across the standalone script and the bot handler.

title: No support_vision flag — image understanding probed by sending ModelImageMessage via generateText
content:
The model info dict (from `model.getInfo()`) has `support_images` which controls image *generation* (output), NOT image *understanding* (input/vision). There is NO `support_vision` or `support_image_understanding` flag in the codebase. Image parsing/understanding is done by calling `model.generateText()` with a `ModelImageMessage` — if the model rejects the request (returns error with keywords like "image", "vision", "multimodal", "content_type", "400"), it's classified as API_REJECT. This means all text-capable models can be probed for image understanding capability by just sending an image message and seeing if they respond or reject it.

This was discovered while creating `scripts/check_image_parsing.py` which probes all text-capable models with a given image file and classifies results based on whether the model accepted the image (PASS), explicitly rejected it (API_REJECT), returned empty text (NO_TEXT), or hit content filters/content moderation (CONTENT_FILTER).

title: Complete LLM tool registration inventory — 4 handlers, 5 tools, parameter details
content:
The LLM has exactly 5 tools registered at runtime, spread across 4 handlers. All registrations happen in handler constructors via `llmService.registerTool(name, description, parameters, handler)`.

| Tool name | Registered by | Parameters |
|---|---|---|
| `get_current_datetime` | CommonHandler (line 74) | none |
| `get_url_content` | CommonHandler (line ~160) | `url` (string, required) |
| `web_search` | YandexSearchHandler (line 127) | `query`, `return_page_content`, `enable_content_filter`, others |
| `get_weather_by_city` | WeatherHandler (line 134) | `city` + `countryCode` |
| `get_weather_by_address` | WeatherHandler (line 159) | `address` + `countryCode` |

Key details per tool:
- All handlers use the flat `registerTool(name, desc, params, handler)` signature — NOT `LLMToolFunction(...)` objects.
- `YandexSearchHandler` and `WeatherHandler` are config-gated (`yandex_search.enabled` / `openweathermap.enabled`). If the config flag is false, the tool is simply never registered.
- `CommonHandler` is always registered.
- Tool execution runs inside `LLMService.generateTextViaLLM()` when `useTools=True`. The service auto-detects tool-call patterns in the model output (both JSON code block and `[TOOL_CALL_START]` markup), executes the handler, appends tool results as `ModelMessage(role="tool")`, and loops.
